english: B. & C., also ske-rágs (ske rags), W. *kye-rágs*, resp. sku-rágs (sku rags) girdle, ska-rags °ćiṅ-ba (ska rags 'ching ba) to put on the girdle, ska-rágs bṡur-ba (ska rags bshur ba) Sch.: a girdle with a clasp (?).
1. Cs.: = kag (kag), keg (keg), mischief; unlucky. — 2. v. rgyu-skar (rgyu skar).
= skoṅ-ba (skong ba); skáṅ-yso (skang gso) 1. satisfaction Sch. — 2. a kind of expiatory sacrifice, to make amends for a duty not performed.

(C.: *ka'*) 1. voice, sound, cry (cf. sgra (sgra)), gláṅ-po-ċe'i skad lta-bu'i sgra (glang po che'i skad lta bu'i sgra) a sound like the voice of an elephant, *ka'-la ċig-pa* dḥọn (don) *mi-ċig-pa* C. (words) equal as to sound, but of different sense (= homophone), sdug-bsṅál-bai skad °byiṅ-pa (sdug bsngal ba'i skad 'bying ba), snyiṅ-rjei skad dón-pa (snying rje'i skad don pa) to utter lamentable cries; skad stėr-ba (skad ster ba) Sch., *ke' gyág-pa* C., *skad táṅ-ċe* W. to sound; *ke' taṅ-wa* C. *skad gyab-ċe* W. ccdp. to call to a person; skad mtún-par (skad mthun par) with one voice, with one accord. — 2. speech, words, talk, news, *ka' ċi naṅ dug* what is your pleasure? what did you say, Sir? C.; zėr-ba de ċi-skad yin (zer ba de ci skad yin) the (words) spoken what speech are they? = what do they mean? Pth.; °di-skad-(du) ('di skad (du)) in these words, thus. (before a literally quoted speech), dė-skad-(ċes) (de skad (ces)) id. (after it); it is also used in a more general sense instead of dė-ltar (de ltar): dė-skad ma byed (de skad ma byed) don't do that Mil.; skad smrá-ba (skad smra ba) to give account, to relate Ld.-Glr. fol. 12. b. Schl., acc. to another reading instead of sra smrás-te (sra smras te); skad byėd-pa (skad byed pa) id., rmi-lám-du byúṅ-ba skad byás-te (rmi lam du byung ba skad byas te) reporting it as having been revealed to him by a dream Pth. — 3. language, bód-skad (bod skad) the Tibetan language, rgya-gár-skad (rgya gar skad) the Sanskrit language, bód-skad-du (bod skad du), col. -la, into or in the Tib. language, yúl-skad-du (yul skad du) into or in the provincial dialect. — 4. a snap with the fingers, always with ċig (cig): skád-ċig-(ma) (skad cig (ma)), gen. as a measure of time: a moment; also adv.: for a moment, skád-ċig-la (skad cig la) in a moment, instantly, in one moment, skád-ċig de-nyíd-la (skad cig de nyid la) in the very same moment. (Some mathematical books compute the skád-ċig (skad cig) = 1/4''', others as long as 1/3''). Comp. and Deriv. skad-°gágs (skad 'gags) hoarseness of the voice, Cs. — skad-ṅán (skad ngan) 1. a bad voice. 2. cry, screaming. — skád-ċan (skad can) having a voice, sounding. — skád-ċa (skad ca) 1. C.: discourse, conversation, *ka'-ċa láb-pa* or *jhė'-pa* to converse, to have a chat. 2. C. talk, rumour, *mii ká'-ċa re* it is (but) talk of the people. 3. W.: news, tidings, intelligence. — skad-ċé (skad che), -ċėn (-chen) 1. a loud voice Sch. 2. n. of an animal Lt. — skad-rnyá (skad rnya) Sch.: a high voice. — skad-snyán sgyúr-ba (skad snyan sgyur ba) Mil. to sing or whistle in a quavering, warbling manner, of birds, flute- players etc., °gyúr-skad ('gyur skad) a singing or playing of this kind. — skad-dód (skad dod) an equivalent word, ċúṅ-mai sk. (chung ma'i skad) another word for wife Gramm. — skád-pa (skad pa) v. the separate article below. — skad-°dzėr (skad 'dzer) Cs. = skad-°gágs (skad 'gags). — skad-bzáṅ (skad bzang) 1. a good voice. 2. W.: good news. — skad-lúgs (skad lugs) dialect. — skad-lóg (skad log) clamour, screaming. — skad-ysáṅs mto (skad gsangs mtho) Sch.: a loud voice, skad-ysáṅ nyams-ċúṅ °gyur (skad gsangs nyams chung 'gyur) the voice is getting weak Wdn.

I. vb. 1. to say, tell, relate, zhiṅ-káms ṡig yód-do skád-par tos (zhing khams shig yod do skad par thos) that a land (of bliss) exists I heard say Mil.; more frq. at the end of a sentence skád-do (skad do) or skad (skad) for: it is said (= dicitur), grags skad (grags skad) id. Mil. — 2. to name, call, skád-pa (skad pa) partic. = byá-ba (bya ba) named, called. — 3. Ld.: *skád-ċe*, *xád-ċe* to measure, take measure. — II. sbs. interpreter; language-master, teacher.
W. instead of ská-ba (ska ba) thick, turbid.

1. time, opportunity, case, circumstances; mtóṅ-(bai) skabs (mthong ba'i skabs) opportunity of seeing, skabs rnyėd-pa (skabs rnyed pa) to find an opportunity, skábs-su (skabs su) or skabs-skábs-su (skabs skabs su) now and then, under certain circumstances,
